replay_command: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/projects/.cargo-target-frankenjax-graymeadow-vmap RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-dispatch --bench dispatch_baseline -- vmap

rows.notes: Matches scoreboard hotspot p95 (60,936ns) within noise. Representative vmap_multiplier proxy: paired i64 vector dispatch over batch=128, width=64.
Within 15% of vmap_dot baseline; control-flow dispatch overhead is modest on top of the underlying multiplier.
Scan dispatch sits ~35% above the vmap_dot baseline; previously optimized cutover keeps this within scan-specific budget.
Already well below the multiplier threshold; recent batched-index switch work paid off (frankenjax-b3xy).
Mid-range; no obvious bottleneck flagged against baseline.
Outlier at ~10ms — ~165x the vmap_dot baseline. Scatter-specific, NOT a generic vmap_multiplier hotspot. Separate scatter follow-up bead recommended.

decision_rationale: Same-worker p95/p99 evidence for the generic vmap_multiplier hotspot (~60-62µs on vmi1227854) matches the scoreboard baseline within noise; no single lever crosses the action threshold for a behavior-preserving change without a sampling profile.

Inspection of BatchTracer::apply_batch_rule (crates/fj-dispatch/src/batching.rs:316-327) shows the all-unbatched fast path allocates a Vec<Value> and Value::clones each input. For tensor inputs the clone is O(N elements) and dominates any single Vec-allocation savings, so a SmallVec micro-rewrite cannot be justified by the current numbers.

vmap_scatter is a 165x outlier but is scatter-specific, not a generic vmap dispatch issue. The scoreboard family is `vmap_multiplier`, so the scatter result must be handled by a separate hotspot row plus its own bead.
Conformance and bench numbers stayed unchanged; no source change committed for this bead.
recommended_next_steps: Open a scatter-specific hotspot bead seeded by vmap_scatter/batched_indices_updates ≈ 10.06ms / ~672k std_dev.

When a sampling profile (perf record + flamegraph or similar) becomes available, capture it for vmap_dot_i64/paired_vectors and re-evaluate whether the Value::clone in the all-unbatched fast path is the dominant frame; only then consider a single lever (SmallVec input buffer, lazy-clone path, or shared-buffer reuse).
Until then, retain the row in optimization_hotspot_scoreboard.v1 and refresh on each cadence.
